messages: Now let me update `routes.jsx` to remove the dead route:
Now let me verify the file was written correctly:
Good. Now let me run the tests to ensure nothing is broken:

All 25 unit tests pass. The 2 "failed" suites are the e2e Playwright specs that can't be loaded by Jest (they import `@playwright/test`) — this is pre-existing and unrelated to my change.  Now let me commit the dead code removal:
The commit was successful. Let me now report the outcome:

## Summary  Successfully completed the **code-cleanup** step for the `product-quick-view` feature.  ### Dead code removed:  1. **`overrides/app/pages/my-new-route/index.jsx`** — Deleted. This was a scaffold placeholder page from the PWA Kit template that rendered only `"hello new route!"` with no business logic or feature purpose.  2. **`overrides/app/routes.jsx`** — Removed the `MyNewRoute` import and `/my-new-route` route entry. The route table now only contains the home page override and the base retail-react-app routes.  ### Verification: - All 25 unit tests pass (product-tile: 8/8, quick-view-modal: 17/17) - No other code referenced `my-new-route` or `MyNewRoute` outside the deleted files - The remaining codebase (quick-view-modal, product-tile, config, e2e tests) contains no dead code — all exports and components are actively consumed
